title: Implement shorts entrypoint (URL -> finished clips)
description: Top-level driver: takes a YouTube URL, runs the full atomic-skill chain (ingest -> transcribe -> detect-faces -> pick-speaker -> pick-segments -> per-span: cut-clip -> reframe-vertical -> burn-subtitles -> loudnorm -> qc-clip -> save-local). Each skill is invoked independently; data passes via JSON on disk under ./work/<id>/. Out of scope: GUI, upload to platforms.

title: Implement yt-dlp ingest skill
description: Download source video locally from a YouTube/URL input. Writes to ./work/<id>/source.mp4 and emits ingest.json with metadata (title, duration, fps, url). First step of pipeline.
close_reason: implemented .claude/skills/ingest/ingest.sh + SKILL.md; verified on Big Buck Bunny URL; produced work/4c772c0f08/source.mp4 (709M) and ingest.json {duration:634.624, fps:60, 3840x2160}

title: Implement pick-segments skill (Claude ranker)
description: Compute per-second RMS energy from source audio. Feed transcript + RMS summary to Claude, get back N spans with t0/t1/rationale/title. See .claude/skills/pick-segments/SKILL.md.
notes: Implemented .claude/skills/pick-segments/{pick-segments.sh,rms.py,build_prompt.py,parse_reply.py}. Calls claude -p (no API key, uses host session). Tested E2E on synthetic 110s transcript with 10 segments: produced 2 non-overlapping spans (0-38.5s 'The Light That Shot Straight Up', 47-95s 'It's Been Visiting For Years') with rationales — Claude correctly avoided mid-thought cuts and respected [20,60]s duration. RMS helper tested on work/4c772c0f08/source.mp4 (10min BBB): 635 buckets, min=6e-05 max=0.093 mean=0.016. Idempotent via mtime cache (cache hit confirmed on second run). claude -p smoke test PONG verified.

title: Implement transcribe skill (whisper.cpp local)
description: Wrap whisper-cli with local GGML model into a Python script. Input video/audio path -> output JSON with words[] and segments[]. Read WHISPER_BIN and WHISPER_MODEL from .env. Extract 16kHz mono via ffmpeg, pipe to whisper-cli with --output-json-full, parse + flatten. See .claude/skills/transcribe/SKILL.md for I/O contract.
close_reason: implemented .claude/skills/transcribe/transcribe.sh; tested on 8.5s say-synthesized speech -> 23 words + 3 segments with word-level timestamps

title: Pivot stack: whisper.cpp + Claude (subscription/crew) + ffmpeg + atomic skills, local output
description: User pivot. Scrap monolithic Python pipeline; modularize every shorts-production op into atomic Claude Code skills under .claude/skills/. Stack: whisper.cpp local (with user's ggml-large-v3-turbo-q5_0.bin model) for transcription, Claude via host Code session + /crew tmux members for semantic ops (no API key — uses subscription), ffmpeg for media, MediaPipe for face boxes, optional Rails+Sidekiq wrapper. Output is local: ./output/<source-name>/. Pre-pivot code preserved on archive/pre-pivot branch.

title: Implement cut-clip skill (ffmpeg trim)
description: Thin ffmpeg wrapper. Stream-copy by default, re-encode flag for frame-accurate cuts. See .claude/skills/cut-clip/SKILL.md.
notes:
Implemented .claude/skills/cut-clip/cut-clip.sh. Tested on work/4c772c0f08/source.mp4 (Big Buck Bunny):
- stream-copy [10,18]: ffprobe duration=9.642667 (keyframe-aligned, expected)
- reencode=true [10,18]: ffprobe duration=8.000000 (frame-accurate)
- re-run with same args: cache hit (mtime check)
Both outputs 3840x2160 video + audio streams intact.

title: Implement burn-subtitles skill (ASS + ffmpeg)
description: Generate ASS subtitle file from word timestamps (line / word-karaoke / selective modes), burn via ffmpeg subtitles= filter. Salvage ASS templating from archive/pre-pivot:pipeline_v2.py:339-512. See .claude/skills/burn-subtitles/SKILL.md.

title: Implement reframe-vertical skill (ffmpeg crop path)
description: Apply speaker-track to source video as smooth 9:16 crop. Compute crop centers, smooth with one-euro filter, render via ffmpeg sendcmd or filter expressions. Salvage smoothing logic from archive/pre-pivot:pipeline_v2.py:178-238. See .claude/skills/reframe-vertical/SKILL.md.

title: Implement detect-faces skill (MediaPipe)
description: Sample frames at configurable fps, run MediaPipe Face Detector, emit per-timestamp boxes JSON. See .claude/skills/detect-faces/SKILL.md.
notes: Tested on a 15s 320x240 talking-head clip (yt-dlp slice of UF8uR6Z6KLc, 30-45s): 75 frames sampled at 5fps, 73 with detected face boxes. First box: x=125 y=40 w=47 h=47 score=0.77. Runtime ~1s. Output schema matches SKILL.md. Idempotent via mtime cache.

title: Implement pick-speaker skill (Claude vision)
description: For each transcript span, identify the active-speaker face box. Single face -> trivial. Multiple faces -> sample 2-3 keyframes + transcript text, ask Claude (host session or /crew member) to pick numbered face. Output speaker_box per span. See .claude/skills/pick-speaker/SKILL.md.

title: Add hello.md with the word "hi"
description:
Dispatched from GitHub issue jperrello/shorts#1. Body:\n\nCreate a new file at the repo root named `hello.md`. Its contents should be the single word: hi

That's all — no other content, no other files. A trailing newline is fine.

title: Implement save-local skill (per-source folder)
description: Read OUTPUT_DIR from .env, mkdir -p <OUTPUT_DIR>/<source-stem>, copy in. Return destination path. See .claude/skills/save-local/SKILL.md.

title: Implement qc-clip skill (ffprobe sanity)
description: ffprobe duration + size. Return verdict JSON with pass bool and reason. See .claude/skills/qc-clip/SKILL.md.

title: Implement loudnorm skill (ffmpeg two-pass)
description: Measure pass + apply pass with measured params. Stream-copy video. Targets I=-14 TP=-1.5 LRA=11 by default. See .claude/skills/loudnorm/SKILL.md.
notes:
Implemented .claude/skills/loudnorm/loudnorm.sh. Two-pass: measure → parse JSON from ffmpeg stderr → apply with measured params (linear=true). Video -c copy, audio -c:a aac -b:a 192k. Idempotent via mtime.

Test: cut-clip on work/4c772c0f08/source.mp4 [30,40] → /tmp/loudnorm_test_in.mp4 → loudnorm → /tmp/loudnorm_test_out.mp4.
Measured input: I=-31.45 TP=-17.46 LRA=8.90 thresh=-41.51 offset=-2.15.
Verification ffmpeg loudnorm on output: input_i=-11.78, input_tp=0.22 (peak limited apply gives output_tp=-1.50 exact). Drift ~2 LU from -14 target on this quiet short clip — within expected two-pass tolerance.

value: Each skill must be independently invocable. Re-running a skill should be idempotent if inputs unchanged. No skill bundles two operations.
Claude-driven skills (pick-segments, pick-speaker) call Claude through the host session or a /crew member, never the API. Inputs: JSON paths. Outputs: JSON to disk.

cut-clip skill works: .claude/skills/cut-clip/cut-clip.sh <input> <t0> <t1> <out> [reencode]. Stream-copy default (keyframe-aligned, ~1s slop), reencode=true gives frame-accurate via libx264 veryfast crf18. Idempotent via mtime cache. Tested on work/4c772c0f08/source.mp4 [10,18]: stream-copy=9.64s, reencode=8.000s exact.

ingest skill works: yt-dlp -> work/<id>/source.mp4 + ingest.json. Tested on Big Buck Bunny (aqz-KE-bpKQ) → 709MB mp4, duration 634.624s, 3840x2160 @ 60fps. id is sha1(url)[:10]. Idempotent: cache-hits when source.mp4 + ingest.json exist for same URL. Script: .claude/skills/ingest/ingest.sh

loudnorm skill works: .claude/skills/loudnorm/loudnorm.sh <input> <out> [I=-14] [TP=-1.5] [LRA=11]. Two-pass ffmpeg loudnorm — measure pass parses JSON from stderr, apply pass uses measured_I/TP/LRA/thresh/offset with linear=true. Video stream-copied, audio reencoded to AAC 192k. Idempotent via mtime cache. Tested on 10s slice of work/4c772c0f08/source.mp4 (BBB intro, quiet -31.45 LUFS): output measured -11.78 LUFS / TP -1.50 dBTP. Short/quiet clips drift ~2 LU from target — expected.
Target output: vertical 9:16 shorts (1080x1920), burned subtitles, loudnorm to broadcast spec, saved under ./out/<source-id>/clip-N.mp4.

One atomic operation per skill. Skills live at .claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md with frontmatter contract. Data between skills passes as JSON files on disk under ./work/<id>/ (transcript.json, faces.json, speaker.json, segments.json).
Stack: whisper.cpp (local transcribe) + Claude (via host session or /crew tmux, NO API key) + ffmpeg + MediaPipe. Binary paths in .env. Never use Anthropic API directly.

transcribe skill works: .claude/skills/transcribe/transcribe.sh <input> [out] [lang]. Uses whisper-cli --max-len 1 --split-on-word --output-json-full for word timestamps; ffmpeg extracts 16kHz mono wav first. Sources .env (WHISPER_BIN, WHISPER_MODEL must be quoted since model path has a space). Tested on synthesized 'say' speech (8.5s) → 23 words + 3 sentence segments. Idempotent via mtime check.

detect-faces skill works: .claude/skills/detect-faces/detect-faces.sh <input> [out] [fps=5]. Wraps detect_faces.py (MediaPipe FaceDetector VIDEO mode with blaze_face_short_range.tflite). Outputs JSON: {source,fps,width,height,duration,frames:[{t,boxes:[{x,y,w,h,score}]}]}. Idempotent via mtime cache. Tested on 15s 320x240 talking-head slice (yt-dlp UF8uR6Z6KLc 30-45s): 75 frames @ 5fps, 73 with faces, ~1s runtime. Note: BBB is animation, no face hits — needs real human faces.

pick-segments skill works: .claude/skills/pick-segments/pick-segments.sh <transcript.json> [out] [n=5] [dmin=20] [dmax=60]. Pipeline: rms.py (ffmpeg s16le 8kHz mono -> per-second RMS) -> build_prompt.py (timestamped transcript + 60-bin sparkline) -> claude -p --output-format text -> parse_reply.py (regex JSON, validate non-overlap+duration). Writes {source, shorts:[{t0,t1,rationale,title_suggestion}]}. Idempotent via mtime. Uses host claude subscription (NO API key). Tested on synthetic 110s/10-segment transcript: 2 valid spans (0-38.5, 47-95). RMS verified on work/4c772c0f08/source.mp4 (635 buckets).
